# maczo Originals — complete merchant integration reference (single file)

This one file contains everything needed to integrate maczo Originals as a seamless aggregator:
the onboarding steps, the wire contract (both call directions + HMAC signing + money model), and the
full OpenAPI specification of the seamless wallet you implement. Intended for one-shot reading by an AI.

Base URLs:  sandbox https://originals-api-dev.maczo.co  ·  production https://originals-api.maczo.co
Portal:     https://portal-dev.maczo.co (sandbox)  ·  https://portal.maczo.co (production)
Docs:       https://docs.maczo.co

================================================================================

# maczo Originals — Integration wire contract

The exact contract to integrate maczo Originals into your platform. You do two things: **call our launch
API** to open a game for a player, and **implement a seamless wallet** that we call to move money during
play. Everything is JSON, signed with HMAC-SHA256 headers, and uses integer minor units.

Machine-readable schema: the **API reference** on this site (Swagger UI). Interactive.

## 1. Roles & directions

- **maczo** = game provider. We run the provably-fair games + UI.
- **you (the operator)** = the source of truth for the player's balance. Our ledger is a display-only
  mirror; we never authorise a bet against a balance we computed — your wallet decides.

```
(1) LAUNCH + CATALOG      you  ──▶  maczo     open a game for a player · sync the game list · end a session
(2) SEAMLESS WALLET       maczo ──▶ you       debit the stake · credit the win · rollback · status · balance
```

## 2. Endpoints

**You call maczo** at your assigned base URL:

| Environment | Base URL |
|---|---|
| Sandbox (dev) | `https://originals-api-dev.maczo.co` |
| Production | `https://originals-api.maczo.co` |

| `POST {base}` | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `/GameLogin` | Open a game session for a player; returns a playable URL |
| `/GetGameList` | Fetch the catalog of games you may offer |
| `/KickPlayer` | End a player's live session(s) |
| `/ReplayLogin` | Open a read-only replay of one settled round (support/disputes) |

These are JSON, signed with the same header HMAC as the wallet callbacks (§3), and always answer HTTP
`200` — classify on the body `code` (`OK` / `OPERATOR_CONFIG_ERROR` / `BAD_REQUEST` /
`SIGNATURE_OR_CLOCK`). The exact request/response bodies are in the **Launch & Catalog API** reference on
this site. `player_ref` is your durable player id (charset `A-Za-z0-9._$?-`, length 1–50) and is echoed
**verbatim** on every wallet callback. `game_code` is an `originals_<id>` value from `/GetGameList`,
passed back to `/GameLogin`.

**maczo calls you** at the wallet callback base URL you register (`{yourWalletBase}{prefix}`):

| `POST` | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `/debit` | Place a bet (idempotent on `txn_ref`) |
| `/credit` | Settle a win (references the debit via `ref_txn_ref`) |
| `/rollback` | Reverse an applied debit |
| `/status` | Read-only: has a `txn_ref` applied? (unknown-result recovery) |
| `/balance` | Display-only balance for a player |

The full request/response schema for the wallet you implement is in the **API reference** on this site.

## 3. Authentication — HMAC-SHA256 headers

Both directions are signed the same way, with the shared secret you exchange with us (versioned by
`X-Key-Id`, so keys can be rotated with an overlap window):

- `X-Signature` — `HMAC_SHA256(secret, canonical)`, lowercase hex
- `X-Timestamp` — unix seconds; within ±60 s of now
- `X-Nonce` — single-use opaque value (reject a seen nonce)
- `X-Key-Id` — selects the signing key

`canonical` is the newline-joined string (no trailing newline):

```
METHOD
PATH
X-Timestamp
X-Nonce
sha256hex(rawBody)      # sha256 of the exact request body bytes; empty body -> sha256("")
```

Reject anything outside the window or with a replayed nonce (fail closed → `SIGNATURE_OR_CLOCK`).

**Rules that catch most integration bugs:**

- `PATH` is the **path only, no query string**. On the wallet callbacks you verify, it is the full path
  **including your prefix** — e.g. `/seamless/debit` (not `/debit`).
- `X-Timestamp` is unix **seconds**, not milliseconds — do **not** use `Date.now()` (13 digits fails
  every time). Keep your clock NTP-synced within 60 s.
- The secret is used as its **raw UTF-8 bytes** — not hex/base64-decoded. `METHOD` is upper-case; the
  signature is lowercase hex; compare in constant time.
- Hash the **raw bytes as received**, before any JSON parsing. Do **not** parse-then-re-serialize to
  compute the body hash — a change in key order or whitespace breaks the signature.
- Money idempotency is always the body `txn_ref`, **never** the transport `X-Nonce`. A retry of the same
  money move carries the **same `txn_ref`** but a **new** nonce/timestamp/signature. You only need to
  remember a nonce for ~2× the freshness window (older ones fail the clock check anyway).

**Worked example** (verify your signer against this exact vector):

```
secret     = "s3cr3t-sample-key"          # raw UTF-8 bytes
METHOD     = POST
PATH       = /seamless/debit
X-Timestamp= 1735732800
X-Nonce    = 9f8e7d6c5b4a39281706f5e4d3c2b1a0
body       = {"operator_id":"acme","player_ref":"pa1b2c3d4_alice","txn_ref":"3f2a...(64 hex)","round_ref":"3f2a...(64 hex)","game_code":"originals_dice","amount_minor":100000,"currency":"THB","currency_exp":2}
sha256hex(body) = 4f378774511b191ff84c8b4a094cf92842bae0d5cec260165a6d8bf2db56a0a0
canonical  = "POST\n/seamless/debit\n1735732800\n9f8e7d6c5b4a39281706f5e4d3c2b1a0\n4f378774511b191ff84c8b4a094cf92842bae0d5cec260165a6d8bf2db56a0a0"
X-Signature= 8193277c573a073a6ed3426c73bf4ddf20b061e90b242e5e7229215cb57d6e0a
```

(An empty body hashes as `sha256("")` = `e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855`.)

## 4. Money model (non-negotiable)

- **A processed request always answers HTTP `200`** — classify the outcome on the body `code`, never on
  the HTTP status. (Two *transport* exceptions on calls **to us**: rate limiting → HTTP `429`
  `{code:"RATE_LIMITED", retryable:true}` with a `Retry-After` header, and an internal error → HTTP
  `500` `{code:"INTERNAL", retryable:true}`. Retry those with backoff. Your wallet, in turn, should
  always answer `200`; we handle your timeouts/5xx as `OPERATOR_TIMEOUT`/`OPERATOR_UNAVAILABLE`.)
- Money is **integer minor units** + `currency` + `currency_exp` (the currency's minor-unit exponent).
  **Never a float.** See §4a for the supported currencies and their exponents.
- **`currency_exp` is present and authoritative on every money callback** — always scale to the exact
  `currency_exp` we send; never hard-code a currency's decimals.
- **Amounts fit a 64-bit integer.** At `currency_exp: 8` (crypto) a balance/turnover can exceed
  JavaScript's `2^53` safe-integer limit — parse and emit every `*_minor` field as a **64-bit integer /
  big-integer**, never as a float or JS `Number`.
- **Idempotency key = `txn_ref`** (we derive it; you never mint it — store it **unique**):
  - same `txn_ref` **and same request** → replay: return `code: DUPLICATE`, `duplicate: true`, and the
    **live** balance in **both** `old_balance_minor` and `new_balance_minor`; move money **once**.
  - same `txn_ref` but a **different** amount/currency/player → `code: IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT`; do **not**
    apply (we send `request_hash` you may use to detect this).
- A `credit` (win) or `rollback` MUST reference the prior applied debit via `ref_txn_ref`; a settle with
  no matching debit returns `code: TXN_NOT_FOUND` (never silent). **A losing round never calls your
  wallet.** A debit is settled by **at most one** `credit` **or** one `rollback` — never both.
- `status(txn_ref)` is a **read-only** query keyed by `txn_ref` alone (no `player_ref`). `txn_ref` is
  globally unique per operator — key your ledger/dedup by `txn_ref`, not per-player — so `status` can
  resolve it. It returns `applied: true|false` and moves no money.
- Unknown / unrecognised `code` ⇒ fail closed (treat as `BAD_REQUEST`).

**Result codes** (you return these on your wallet; classify on `code`): `OK`, `DUPLICATE`,
`INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS`, `SESSION_INVALID`, `SESSION_REPLACED`, `PLAYER_BLOCKED`, `ALREADY_SETTLED`,
`IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT`, `TXN_NOT_FOUND`, `OPERATOR_TIMEOUT`, `OPERATOR_UNAVAILABLE`, `RATE_LIMITED`,
`BAD_REQUEST`, `OPERATOR_CONFIG_ERROR`, `SIGNATURE_OR_CLOCK`.

## 4a. Currencies, settlement & limits

**Settlement currency — the key fact.** We call your wallet (`debit`/`credit`/`rollback`/`balance`) in the
**exact currency you launch the session with** — your enabled currency (e.g. **THB**, or **USDT**). There
is **no FX on the wire**: `amount_minor` is in your currency's minor units at its `currency_exp`, and every
response you return is in that same currency/exponent. You do not receive USD and you do not convert.

**Supported currencies + exponents** (maczo is authoritative — for a supported currency you may omit
`currency_exp` at launch; we ignore a supplied one and stamp the authoritative value on every callback):

| Kind | `currency` codes | `currency_exp` |
|---|---|---|
| Fiat (2 dp) | USD, EUR, GBP, THB, SGD, MYR, PHP, INR, IDR, BRL, MXN, AUD, CNY, HKD | `2` |
| Fiat (0 dp) | JPY, KRW, VND | `0` |
| Crypto / stablecoin | USDT, USDC, DAI, BTC, ETH, BNB, SOL, TRX, TON, XRP, DOGE, LTC | `8` |

`currency` is the **ISO-4217 code** for fiat and maczo's **symbol** for crypto (e.g. `USDT`, `BTC`, `ETH`
— not ISO). The **0-dp fiats are a common trap**: `amount_minor: 500` in JPY is ¥500 (not ¥5). Your
enabled currencies are provisioned during onboarding (see the Onboarding guide) — a launch with a currency
outside your enabled set is rejected `BAD_REQUEST`.

**Examples across exponents** (the launch currency flows unchanged into every callback):

```
฿1,000.00 THB (exp 2)  ->  { "amount_minor": 100000,        "currency": "THB",  "currency_exp": 2 }
¥1,000    JPY (exp 0)  ->  { "amount_minor": 1000,          "currency": "JPY",  "currency_exp": 0 }
0.00100000 BTC (exp 8) ->  { "amount_minor": 100000,        "currency": "BTC",  "currency_exp": 8 }
12.34567890 USDT       ->  { "amount_minor": 1234567890,    "currency": "USDT", "currency_exp": 8 }
```

**Bet limits.** Each game has a min-bet, max-bet, and max-win, enforced **per settlement currency**
(auto-derived, RTP-safe, floored to the currency's exponent) — a bet below the min is rejected
`BET_TOO_SMALL`, above the max `BET_TOO_LARGE`, and a win above the cap is clipped. You don't configure
these per currency; the effective values are visible in the merchant portal.

## 5. Ordering guarantee

Every bet follows a strict order so money is never at risk: validate the session → **debit your wallet
and confirm it applied** → draw the provably-fair outcome → commit the round → credit any win → reveal
the result to the player. A rejected or unfunded bet never draws an outcome, and a win is never shown for
an unfunded bet.

**Settlement can lag / be retried.** A `credit` or `rollback` for a debit may arrive seconds-to-minutes
after the debit and out of band from live play (a failed settle is parked and retried by reconciliation
until you acknowledge it). Honour a `credit`/`rollback` against its referenced debit **with no expiry**,
and rely on `txn_ref` idempotency for the retries. A single debit is resolved by **at most one** `credit`
**or** one `rollback` — never both.

## 6. Provably fair

Every result is re-derivable from `(server_seed, client_seed, nonce)` via
`HMAC-SHA256(server_seed, "client_seed:nonce:cursor")`. The server-seed hash is committed before use and
revealed on rotation, so any player or you can independently verify any round.

## 7. Signing keys & the shared secret

- **You generate the secret** (e.g. 32 random bytes as hex) and send it to us **out-of-band** (never on
  the wire). We register your `operator_id` and a `key_id`; you present that `key_id` as `X-Key-Id`.
- The secret is used as its **raw UTF-8 bytes** in `HMAC_SHA256(secret, canonical)` — we do **not**
  hex- or base64-decode it first. Whatever string we exchange is the key material verbatim.
- **Rotation is two-directional.** We can hold more than one active key for you. On calls you sign to us,
  present the new `key_id` while the old one still verifies during the overlap. On the wallet callbacks
  **we** sign to you, keep **accepting both `key_id`s** (map `X-Key-Id` → the right secret) until we
  confirm cut-over, then retire the old secret.

## 8. Callback registration

You register (in the portal, or we set it at onboarding) your **wallet callback base URL** and a **path
prefix** (default `/seamless`). We call `{base}{prefix}/debit`, `/credit`, etc. The signed `PATH` in the
HMAC canonical is exactly **`{prefix}/{func}`** (e.g. `/seamless/debit`) — the scheme/host are **not**
signed, so your prefix must match on both sides. You also register an inbound **IP allowlist** for the
hosts that call our launch API.

## 9. Go-live checklist

1. We register your operator + `key_id`; you send your secret out-of-band (§7).
2. Implement the five wallet endpoints (Seamless Wallet API) and verify our signature (§3).
3. Set your wallet callback base URL + path prefix + inbound IP allowlist (§8).
4. Test on the **sandbox** (`https://originals-api-dev.maczo.co`) end-to-end: `/GameLogin` → open the
   returned URL → place a bet → your wallet receives `debit` then `credit`/no-call-on-loss → reveal.
   Confirm your wallet honours idempotency (`txn_ref`), insufficient-funds, and `status`.
5. We approve go-live → your operator flips to active on production.

See **Onboarding** on this site for the step-by-step.

================================================================================

# Onboarding — sandbox → production

How to connect your platform to maczo Originals as a seamless aggregator. Full contract:
**Integration wire contract** on this site; interactive schema: the **API reference**.

## Endpoints

| Surface | Sandbox (dev) | Production |
|---|---|---|
| You → maczo (launch / catalog / kick) | `https://originals-api-dev.maczo.co` | `https://originals-api.maczo.co` |
| Merchant portal (your reports + settings) | `https://portal-dev.maczo.co` | `https://portal.maczo.co` |
| These docs | `https://docs.maczo.co` | `https://docs.maczo.co` |

## What you provide / what maczo provides

| maczo provides | you provide |
|---|---|
| `operator_id`, a signing `key_id`, the launch/catalog endpoints, the games catalog | Your **wallet callback base URL** + path prefix (`{yourWalletBase}{prefix}`) that we call |
| The wire contract + interactive API reference + a sandbox to test against | Your **signing secret** (you generate it; exchanged out-of-band, never on the wire) |
| Your **enabled currencies** provisioned (+ we confirm each currency's minor-unit exponent) | The **currencies** you want enabled + your **base currency** (settlement currency) |
| Sandbox + a go-live approval | Your inbound **IP allowlist** (hosts that call our launch API) |

### The shared secret (important)

**You generate the HMAC secret** (e.g. `openssl rand -hex 32`) and send it to us out-of-band. maczo
issues the `operator_id` + `key_id`; the secret value never travels on the wire and is not entered
through any UI. It is used as its **raw UTF-8 bytes** in `HMAC_SHA256(secret, canonical)` — not
hex/base64-decoded. maczo provisions your secret before your operator can go active; until then wallet
calls fail closed with `OPERATOR_CONFIG_ERROR`. Keys can be rotated with an overlap window (`X-Key-Id`).

### Currencies & settlement (important)

You **settle in your own currency** — every wallet callback is denominated in the currency you launch
with, with **no FX on the wire**. During onboarding you tell us which currencies to enable (e.g. `THB`,
or `USDT`) and your base currency; we confirm each one's authoritative minor-unit exponent. Supported:
fiat 2 dp (`USD EUR GBP THB SGD MYR PHP INR IDR BRL MXN AUD CNY HKD`), fiat 0 dp (`JPY KRW VND`), crypto
8 dp (`USDT USDC DAI BTC ETH BNB SOL TRX TON XRP DOGE LTC`). Amounts are integer minor units at that
exponent — always read `currency_exp` from each callback; parse `*_minor` as a 64-bit integer (exp-8
amounts can exceed `2^53`). Per-currency bet limits are derived automatically (RTP-safe).

## Steps

1. **Register.** We onboard your operator and issue your `operator_id` + a signing `key_id`, and enable
   your currencies (base + the set you requested). You receive portal credentials.
2. **Implement the seamless wallet** — the five endpoints in the **Seamless Wallet API**, verifying our
   HMAC-SHA256 header signature. Then call our **Launch & Catalog API** (`/GameLogin`, `/GetGameList`,
   `/KickPlayer`, `/ReplayLogin`) signed the same way — one signing routine for both directions.
3. **Configure your side** in the portal: your wallet callback base URL + **path prefix** (default
   `/seamless`; the signed path is `{prefix}/{func}`) + your inbound IP allowlist.
4. **Test on the sandbox** — point at `https://originals-api-dev.maczo.co`: `/GameLogin` → open the
   returned URL → place a bet → your wallet receives `debit`, then `credit` on a win (a loss never calls
   your wallet). Confirm idempotency (a replayed `txn_ref` returns `duplicate:true`, money moves once),
   insufficient-funds, and the read-only `status(txn_ref)`. **Confirm the `debit`/`credit` arrive in your
   launch currency with the expected `currency_exp`, and that `amount_minor` matches the stake placed.**
   Your wallet is the source of the balance, so you fund the test player on your side (maczo never mints).
5. **Go live.** We approve production; your operator flips to active and your callback host is enabled
   for production traffic.
6. **Reconcile.** Use the portal reports (GGR / turnover / RTP-actual / settlement) against your own
   ledger; commission statements are produced per your agreed basis.

## What you can configure in the portal

- **Integration:** your wallet callback base URL + path prefix, inbound IP allowlist, signing keys.
- **Games:** the subset of the catalogue you offer (`GetGameList` returns only your enabled games).
- **Currencies:** the subset of supported currencies you accept (within what we enabled for you).
- **Limits:** you may **tighten** per-game limits only — raise the min bet, lower the max bet / max win;
  you can never loosen the platform limits.
- **Commercial:** your commission basis + rate (billing figure computed from aggregated totals).

## What you can rely on

- **You are the source of truth for money** — we never authorise a bet on a computed balance. Every
  money move is idempotent on a server-derived `txn_ref` (both sides dedupe).
- **Strict ordering** — the stake is confirmed applied before any outcome is drawn or revealed; a losing
  round never calls your wallet.
- Every outcome is **provably fair** and independently verifiable. Commission is a billing figure
  computed from aggregated totals — never deducted from a player's payout.

================================================================================

# OpenAPI specification — maczo-native-seamless.v1.yaml

```yaml
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: maczo Originals — Seamless Wallet API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: >-
    The seamless wallet contract you (the operator) implement and maczo calls to move money during play.


    Direction: **maczo (provider) → you (operator)**. You hold the real player balance and are the source
    of truth; maczo's ledger is a display-only mirror.


    **Money-safety invariants (non-negotiable):**

    - HTTP status is ALWAYS `200`. Classify the outcome on the body `code`, never on the HTTP status.

    - **Settlement currency:** we call your wallet in the **exact currency you launched the session with**
      (your enabled currency, e.g. THB or USDT) — there is **no FX on the wire**. `amount_minor` is in
      that currency's minor units; every response you return is in the same `currency`/`currency_exp`.

    - Money is **integer minor units** + `currency` + `currency_exp`. **Never a float.** `currency` is the
      ISO-4217 code for fiat and maczo's symbol for crypto (`USDT`, `BTC`, …). `currency_exp` is present
      and **authoritative on every callback** — scale to the exact value we send; never hard-code decimals.
      Supported: fiat `USD EUR GBP THB SGD MYR PHP INR IDR BRL MXN AUD CNY HKD` = exp **2**;
      `JPY KRW VND` = exp **0** (a 0-dp trap: `amount_minor:500` JPY is ¥500); crypto
      `USDT USDC DAI BTC ETH BNB SOL TRX TON XRP DOGE LTC` = exp **8**.

    - **Amounts fit int64.** At exp 8 a balance can exceed `2^53` — parse/emit every `*_minor` as a 64-bit
      integer / big-integer, never a float or JS `Number`.

    - Bet min/max and max-win are enforced **per settlement currency** (auto-derived, RTP-safe); a win
      above the cap is clipped before `credit`.

    - Idempotency key = `txn_ref`. A duplicate returns `code: DUPLICATE`, `duplicate: true`, and the
      **LIVE** balance — it never re-moves money.

    - A `credit` (win) or `rollback` MUST reference a prior applied debit via `ref_txn_ref`; a settle
      with no prior debit returns `code: TXN_NOT_FOUND` (never silent).

    - `status` is a REAL read-only query of whether a `txn_ref` applied — it MUST NOT move money.

    - Unknown / unrecognised `code` ⇒ fail closed (treated as `BAD_REQUEST`, never blind-retried).


    **Authentication (header HMAC-SHA256).** Every request carries `X-Signature` (lowercase hex),
    `X-Timestamp`, `X-Nonce`, `X-Key-Id`. `X-Signature = HMAC_SHA256(secret, canonical)` where `canonical`
    is the newline-joined string (no trailing newline): `METHOD` (upper-case) + `PATH` + `X-Timestamp` +
    `X-Nonce` + `sha256hex(rawBody)`. Verify rules (you VERIFY these on your wallet — get them exact):

    - `PATH` is the **full request path including your prefix** — e.g. `/seamless/debit` — with **no query
      string**.

    - The secret is your out-of-band shared key used as its **raw UTF-8 bytes** (not hex/base64-decoded),
      selected by `X-Key-Id` (rotate with an overlap window — accept both key ids until we cut over).

    - `sha256hex(rawBody)` is over the **exact bytes received** (do not parse-then-re-serialize); an empty
      body hashes as `sha256("")` = `e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855`.

    - `X-Timestamp` is unix **seconds** (not milliseconds), within **±60 s**; `X-Nonce` is single-use.
      Compare in constant time; reject stale/replayed/bad → fail closed (`SIGNATURE_OR_CLOCK`).

    `X-Signature` is a **per-request computed HMAC**, not a static credential — a generated client needs a
    signing hook, not a fixed header value.
servers:
  - url: https://{merchantWalletHost}{pathPrefix}
    description: The merchant's seamless wallet base URL + path prefix (from the operators registry).
    variables:
      merchantWalletHost:
        default: wallet.example.com
      pathPrefix:
        default: /seamless
security:
  - nativeSignature: []
paths:
  /debit:
    post:
      summary: Debit (place a bet). Idempotent on txn_ref.
      operationId: debit
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigTimestamp"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigNonce"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigKeyId"
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/DebitRequest" }
            example:
              operator_id: gpx_dev
              player_ref: pa1b2c3d4_alice
              txn_ref: 3f2a9c…(64 hex)
              round_ref: 3f2a9c…(64 hex)
              game_code: originals_dice
              amount_minor: 100000
              currency: THB
              currency_exp: 2
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Always 200; the outcome (success, DUPLICATE replay, or a reject) is in the body.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/MoneyResponse" }
              examples:
                success:
                  value: { status: ok, code: OK, duplicate: false, old_balance_minor: 5000000, new_balance_minor: 4900000, currency: THB, currency_exp: 2, operator_ref: "your-ledger-id" }
                duplicate:
                  value: { status: ok, code: DUPLICATE, duplicate: true, old_balance_minor: 4900000, new_balance_minor: 4900000, currency: THB, currency_exp: 2, operator_ref: "your-ledger-id" }
                insufficient_funds:
                  value: { status: error, code: INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS, currency: THB, currency_exp: 2, message: "balance too low" }
  /credit:
    post:
      summary: Credit (settle a win). Only called when payout_minor > 0. Requires ref_txn_ref.
      operationId: credit
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigTimestamp"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigNonce"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigKeyId"
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/CreditRequest" }
            example:
              operator_id: gpx_dev
              player_ref: pa1b2c3d4_alice
              txn_ref: 3f2a9c…:WIN
              ref_txn_ref: 3f2a9c…(the debit txn_ref)
              round_ref: 3f2a9c…(64 hex)
              game_code: originals_dice
              payout_minor: 250000
              valid_bet_minor: 100000
              currency: THB
              currency_exp: 2
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Always 200; outcome in the body. TXN_NOT_FOUND if ref_txn_ref has no applied debit.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/MoneyResponse" }
              examples:
                success:
                  value: { status: ok, code: OK, duplicate: false, old_balance_minor: 4900000, new_balance_minor: 5150000, currency: THB, currency_exp: 2, operator_ref: "your-ledger-id" }
                no_prior_debit:
                  value: { status: error, code: TXN_NOT_FOUND, currency: THB, currency_exp: 2, message: "no applied debit for ref_txn_ref" }
  /rollback:
    post:
      summary: Rollback (reverse an applied debit). amount_minor MUST equal the original debit.
      operationId: rollback
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigTimestamp"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigNonce"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigKeyId"
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/RollbackRequest" }
            example:
              operator_id: gpx_dev
              player_ref: pa1b2c3d4_alice
              txn_ref: 3f2a9c…:RB
              ref_txn_ref: 3f2a9c…(the debit txn_ref)
              round_ref: 3f2a9c…(64 hex)
              amount_minor: 100000
              currency: THB
              currency_exp: 2
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Always 200; outcome is in the body. amount_minor MUST equal the original debit.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/MoneyResponse" }
              example: { status: ok, code: OK, duplicate: false, old_balance_minor: 4900000, new_balance_minor: 5000000, currency: THB, currency_exp: 2, operator_ref: "your-ledger-id" }
  /status:
    post:
      summary: Read-only probe of whether a txn_ref applied (unknown-result recovery). Moves no money.
      operationId: status
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigTimestamp"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigNonce"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigKeyId"
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/StatusRequest" }
            example: { operator_id: gpx_dev, txn_ref: 3f2a9c…(64 hex) }
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Always 200. Keyed by txn_ref alone (globally unique per operator); moves no money.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/StatusResponse" }
              examples:
                applied:
                  value: { status: ok, code: OK, applied: true, old_balance_minor: 5000000, new_balance_minor: 4900000, currency: THB, currency_exp: 2 }
                not_found:
                  value: { status: ok, code: TXN_NOT_FOUND, applied: false, currency: THB, currency_exp: 2 }
  /balance:
    post:
      summary: Display-only balance for a player. Never used to authorize a bet.
      operationId: balance
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigTimestamp"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigNonce"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigKeyId"
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/BalanceRequest" }
            example: { operator_id: gpx_dev, player_ref: pa1b2c3d4_alice, currency: THB }
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Always 200. Display-only — never used to authorise a bet.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/BalanceResponse" }
              example: { status: ok, code: OK, balance_minor: 5000000, currency: THB, currency_exp: 2 }
components:
  securitySchemes:
    nativeSignature:
      type: apiKey
      in: header
      name: X-Signature
      description: >-
        HMAC-SHA256 hex over METHOD+PATH+X-Timestamp+X-Nonce+sha256hex(body). Accompanied by the
        X-Timestamp, X-Nonce and X-Key-Id headers (see parameters). NOTE: this is a **per-request
        computed** signature, not a static credential — a generated client must supply a signing hook
        (see the description for the exact canonical string + a worked vector in the wire contract guide).
  parameters:
    SigTimestamp:
      name: X-Timestamp
      in: header
      required: true
      schema: { type: string }
      description: Unix seconds (integer string); within ±60 s of now.
    SigNonce:
      name: X-Nonce
      in: header
      required: true
      schema: { type: string }
      description: Single-use opaque nonce.
    SigKeyId:
      name: X-Key-Id
      in: header
      required: true
      schema: { type: string }
      description: Selects the per-merchant signing key (rotation).
  schemas:
    Money:
      type: object
      required: [operator_id, player_ref, txn_ref, currency, currency_exp]
      properties:
        operator_id: { type: string, example: gpx_dev }
        player_ref:
          type: string
          description: Your durable player identity, echoed verbatim (charset A-Za-z0-9._$?-, length 1–50).
          example: pa1b2c3d4_alice
        txn_ref:
          type: string
          description: Server-derived idempotency key for this money move (64-hex; you never mint it). Store UNIQUE; globally unique per operator.
          example: 3f2a9c…(64 hex)
        round_ref:
          type: string
          description: Round identity. credit + rollback echo it byte-identically from the debit; equals the debit txn_ref for single-shot rounds.
          example: 3f2a9c…(64 hex)
        game_code: { type: string, description: "originals_<id> (from GetGameList).", example: originals_dice }
        currency: { type: string, description: "ISO-4217 for fiat, maczo symbol for crypto (USDT/BTC/…); the session's settlement currency.", example: THB }
        currency_exp: { type: integer, description: Authoritative minor-unit exponent (read per-call; do not hard-code)., example: 2 }
        request_hash: { type: string, description: "Advisory: a hash of the money request; you may use it to detect an IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT (same txn_ref, different body)." }
        meta: { type: object, additionalProperties: true }
    DebitRequest:
      allOf:
        - $ref: "#/components/schemas/Money"
        - type: object
          required: [amount_minor]
          properties:
            amount_minor: { type: integer, format: int64, minimum: 1, description: "Integer minor units (>0) in `currency` at `currency_exp`. Parse as int64/bigint — exp-8 amounts can exceed 2^53." }
    CreditRequest:
      allOf:
        - $ref: "#/components/schemas/Money"
        - type: object
          required: [payout_minor, ref_txn_ref]
          properties:
            payout_minor: { type: integer, format: int64, minimum: 1, description: "Win amount in minor units (>0). Credit is win-only — a losing round never calls your wallet." }
            valid_bet_minor: { type: integer, format: int64, minimum: 0, description: Turnover-eligible stake. }
            ref_txn_ref: { type: string, description: The prior applied debit's txn_ref (round_ref echoes it)., example: 3f2a9c…(64 hex) }
    RollbackRequest:
      allOf:
        - $ref: "#/components/schemas/Money"
        - type: object
          required: [amount_minor, ref_txn_ref]
          properties:
            amount_minor: { type: integer, format: int64, minimum: 1, description: MUST equal the original debit (minor units). }
            ref_txn_ref: { type: string }
    StatusRequest:
      type: object
      required: [operator_id, txn_ref]
      properties:
        operator_id: { type: string }
        txn_ref: { type: string }
    BalanceRequest:
      type: object
      required: [operator_id, player_ref, currency]
      properties:
        operator_id: { type: string }
        player_ref: { type: string }
        currency: { type: string }
    MoneyResponse:
      type: object
      required: [status, code, currency, currency_exp]
      properties:
        status: { type: string, enum: [ok, error] }
        code:
          type: string
          description: Canonical WalletCode.
          enum: [OK, DUPLICATE, INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS, SESSION_INVALID, SESSION_REPLACED, PLAYER_BLOCKED,
                 ALREADY_SETTLED, IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT, TXN_NOT_FOUND, OPERATOR_TIMEOUT,
                 OPERATOR_UNAVAILABLE, RATE_LIMITED, BAD_REQUEST, OPERATOR_CONFIG_ERROR, SIGNATURE_OR_CLOCK]
        duplicate: { type: boolean, description: "Idempotent replay — set true and return the LIVE balance in BOTH old_balance_minor and new_balance_minor." }
        old_balance_minor: { type: integer, format: int64 }
        new_balance_minor: { type: integer, format: int64 }
        currency: { type: string, description: Must equal the request's currency. }
        currency_exp: { type: integer, description: Must equal the request's currency_exp. }
        operator_ref: { type: string, description: "Merchant-side id, for the ledger join." }
        message: { type: string }
    StatusResponse:
      type: object
      required: [status, applied, currency, currency_exp]
      properties:
        status: { type: string, enum: [ok, error] }
        code: { type: string }
        applied: { type: boolean, description: Whether this txn_ref is durably applied. }
        old_balance_minor: { type: integer, format: int64, nullable: true }
        new_balance_minor: { type: integer, format: int64, nullable: true }
        currency: { type: string }
        currency_exp: { type: integer }
    BalanceResponse:
      type: object
      required: [status, currency, currency_exp]
      properties:
        status: { type: string, enum: [ok, error] }
        code: { type: string }
        balance_minor: { type: integer, format: int64, description: Present on success; in `currency` at `currency_exp`. }
        currency: { type: string }
        currency_exp: { type: integer }
        message: { type: string }
```

# OpenAPI specification — maczo-launch.v1.yaml

```yaml
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: maczo Originals — Launch & Catalog API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: >-
    The endpoints **you (the operator) call on maczo** to open a game for a player and to sync the game
    catalogue. This is the inbound half of the integration; the money half (maczo → your wallet) is the
    separate **Seamless Wallet API**.


    Direction: **you (operator) → maczo (provider)**. Every call is JSON and is signed with the SAME
    header-HMAC scheme as the wallet callbacks (see *Authentication*), so you implement one signing
    routine for both directions.


    **Conventions (non-negotiable):**

    - A processed request answers HTTP `200` — classify the outcome on the body `code`. Two *transport*
      exceptions: rate limiting → HTTP `429` `{code:"RATE_LIMITED", retryable:true}` with a `Retry-After`
      header, and an internal error → HTTP `500` `{code:"INTERNAL", retryable:true}`. Retry those with
      backoff.

    - `player_ref` is YOUR durable player identity (charset `A-Za-z0-9._$?-`, length 1–50). maczo binds
      it to the game session and echoes it **verbatim** on every wallet callback (`player_ref`). maczo
      never invents or transforms a player id.

    - `game_code` is exactly a value returned by `/GetGameList` (`originals_<id>`), passed back to
      `/GameLogin`. There is one product (`originals`).

    - **Settlement currency:** launch with your enabled currency (`currency`); every subsequent wallet
      callback settles in **that** currency (no FX). Supported: fiat (2 dp) `USD EUR GBP THB SGD MYR PHP
      INR IDR BRL MXN AUD CNY HKD`; fiat (0 dp) `JPY KRW VND`; crypto (8 dp) `USDT USDC DAI BTC ETH BNB
      SOL TRX TON XRP DOGE LTC`. maczo is authoritative for the exponent of a supported currency — you may
      omit `currency_exp` and every callback carries the exact value to use. Your enabled currencies are
      provisioned at onboarding; a currency outside your enabled set is rejected `BAD_REQUEST`.

    - Money is settled by the wallet callbacks in integer minor units; nothing here moves money.


    **Authentication (header HMAC-SHA256).** Identical to the Seamless Wallet API. Each request carries
    `X-Signature` (lowercase hex of `HMAC_SHA256(secret, canonical)`), `X-Timestamp` (unix seconds,
    within ±60 s), `X-Nonce` (single-use), and `X-Key-Id` (selects your signing key, for rotation).
    `canonical` is the newline-joined string `METHOD` + `PATH` + `X-Timestamp` + `X-Nonce` +
    `sha256hex(rawBody)`. `PATH` is the path only (no query string); an empty body hashes as
    `sha256("")`. The secret is used as its raw UTF-8 bytes (not hex/base64-decoded). Reject-and-fail
    closed outside the window or on a replayed nonce (`code: SIGNATURE_OR_CLOCK`).
servers:
  - url: https://originals-api-dev.maczo.co
    description: Sandbox (dev)
  - url: https://originals-api.maczo.co
    description: Production
security:
  - nativeSignature: []
paths:
  /GameLogin:
    post:
      summary: Open a game session for a player; returns a playable URL.
      operationId: gameLogin
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigTimestamp"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigNonce"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigKeyId"
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/GameLoginRequest" }
            example:
              operator_id: gpx_dev
              player_ref: pa1b2c3d4_alice
              currency: THB
              game_code: originals_dice
              platform: WEB
              lobby_url: https://operator.example/lobby
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Always 200; outcome is in the body.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/LaunchResponse" }
              example: { status: ok, code: OK, url: "https://originals-dev.maczo.co/embed/originals_dice?session=eyJ..." }
  /GetGameList:
    post:
      summary: Fetch the catalogue of games you may offer.
      operationId: getGameList
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigTimestamp"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigNonce"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigKeyId"
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/GetGameListRequest" }
            example: { operator_id: gpx_dev }
      responses:
        "200":
          description: >-
            Always 200; the catalogue is in `games`. Returns only the games ENABLED for your operator
            (per-operator + dynamic — re-sync periodically, do not hard-code the list).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/GameListResponse" }
              example:
                status: ok
                code: OK
                games:
                  - game_code: originals_dice
                    name: Dice
                    category: ORIGINALS
                    provider: originals
                    image_url: https://originals-api-dev.maczo.co/img/tiles/dice.png?v=ab12cd34ef
                    active: true
                    has_demo: true
                    interactive: false
                    live: false
  /KickPlayer:
    post:
      summary: End a player's live session(s). A fresh launch after a kick works normally.
      operationId: kickPlayer
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigTimestamp"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigNonce"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigKeyId"
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/KickPlayerRequest" }
            example: { operator_id: gpx_dev, player_ref: pa1b2c3d4_alice }
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Always 200.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/SimpleResponse" }
              example: { status: ok, code: OK }
  /ReplayLogin:
    post:
      summary: Open a read-only replay of one settled round (support / disputes).
      operationId: replayLogin
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigTimestamp"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigNonce"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/SigKeyId"
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/ReplayLoginRequest" }
            example:
              operator_id: gpx_dev
              player_ref: pa1b2c3d4_alice
              round_ref: "a1b2c3d4e5f6...(the round_ref maczo returned on settle)"
              game_code: originals_dice
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Always 200; a read-only replay URL (never a betting session).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/LaunchResponse" }
              example: { status: ok, code: OK, url: "https://originals-dev.maczo.co/embed/originals_dice?replay=eyJ..." }
components:
  securitySchemes:
    nativeSignature:
      type: apiKey
      in: header
      name: X-Signature
      description: >-
        HMAC-SHA256 hex over METHOD+PATH+X-Timestamp+X-Nonce+sha256hex(body). Accompanied by
        X-Timestamp, X-Nonce and X-Key-Id (see parameters). Same scheme as the Seamless Wallet API.
  parameters:
    SigTimestamp:
      name: X-Timestamp
      in: header
      required: true
      schema: { type: string }
      description: Unix seconds (integer string); within ±60 s of now.
    SigNonce:
      name: X-Nonce
      in: header
      required: true
      schema: { type: string }
      description: Single-use opaque nonce.
    SigKeyId:
      name: X-Key-Id
      in: header
      required: true
      schema: { type: string }
      description: Selects your signing key (rotation).
  schemas:
    GameLoginRequest:
      type: object
      required: [operator_id, player_ref]
      properties:
        operator_id: { type: string, description: Your maczo operator id., example: gpx_dev }
        player_ref:
          type: string
          description: Your durable player identity (charset A-Za-z0-9._$?-, length 1–50). Echoed verbatim on every wallet callback.
          example: pa1b2c3d4_alice
        currency:
          type: string
          description: >-
            Your settlement currency for this session (must be one of your enabled currencies; defaults to
            your base currency). Every wallet callback settles in this currency. See the supported list in
            the API description.
          example: THB
        game_code:
          type: string
          description: An originals_<id> value from GetGameList. Omit to open a default game.
          example: originals_dice
        language: { type: string, description: "Optional UI language hint (e.g. th, en).", example: th }
        platform: { type: string, enum: [WEB, MOBILE], example: WEB }
        lobby_url: { type: string, nullable: true, description: Return/lobby URL., example: https://operator.example/lobby }
        demo: { type: boolean, description: "Open a free fun-play session (no wallet, no money path).", default: false }
        currency_exp:
          type: integer
          description: >-
            Advanced/optional. For a supported currency maczo uses its authoritative exponent and IGNORES
            this; you normally omit it. (Only consulted for a currency outside the supported table.)
          example: 2
        exchange_rate:
          type: string
          nullable: true
          description: Advanced/optional display rate — not used for native settlement; normally omit.
        meta: { type: object, additionalProperties: true, description: Optional passthrough. }
    GetGameListRequest:
      type: object
      required: [operator_id]
      properties:
        operator_id: { type: string, example: gpx_dev }
    KickPlayerRequest:
      type: object
      required: [operator_id, player_ref]
      properties:
        operator_id: { type: string, example: gpx_dev }
        player_ref: { type: string, example: pa1b2c3d4_alice }
    ReplayLoginRequest:
      type: object
      required: [operator_id, player_ref, round_ref]
      properties:
        operator_id: { type: string, example: gpx_dev }
        player_ref: { type: string, example: pa1b2c3d4_alice }
        round_ref:
          type: string
          description: The round_ref maczo returned on settle (equals the bet's txn_ref for single-shot rounds).
        game_code: { type: string, description: The round's game (originals_<id>)., example: originals_dice }
    LaunchResponse:
      type: object
      required: [status, code]
      properties:
        status: { type: string, enum: [ok, error] }
        code: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/LaunchCode" }
        url: { type: string, nullable: true, description: Player-facing game/replay URL (present when code=OK). }
        message: { type: string, nullable: true }
    GameListResponse:
      type: object
      required: [status, code, games]
      properties:
        status: { type: string, enum: [ok, error] }
        code: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/LaunchCode" }
        games:
          type: array
          items: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/GameItem" }
    SimpleResponse:
      type: object
      required: [status, code]
      properties:
        status: { type: string, enum: [ok, error] }
        code: { $ref: "#/components/schemas/LaunchCode" }
        message: { type: string, nullable: true }
    GameItem:
      type: object
      required: [game_code, name, category, image_url, active]
      properties:
        game_code: { type: string, description: "Slug `originals_<lowercase-hyphenated-id>`; the sole value you pass back to /GameLogin & /ReplayLogin.", example: originals_dice }
        name: { type: string, example: Dice }
        category: { type: string, example: ORIGINALS }
        provider: { type: string, example: originals }
        image_url: { type: string, description: Cover tile (versioned; safe to cache by URL)., example: https://originals-api-dev.maczo.co/img/tiles/dice.png?v=ab12cd34ef }
        active: { type: boolean, example: true }
        has_demo: { type: boolean, example: true }
        interactive: { type: boolean, description: Multi-step game (start/step/cashout)., example: false }
        live: { type: boolean, description: "Shared-timed multiplayer round (none currently offered — always false).", example: false }
    LaunchCode:
      type: string
      description: >-
        Outcome code. `OK` = success. `OPERATOR_CONFIG_ERROR` = unknown/suspended operator or inbound IP
        not allowlisted. `BAD_REQUEST` = invalid player_ref / unknown game_code / currency not enabled.
        `SIGNATURE_OR_CLOCK` = bad/stale signature, timestamp outside window, or replayed nonce.
        `RATE_LIMITED` = too many requests (returned with HTTP 429 + Retry-After; retry with backoff).
        `INTERNAL` = transient server error (HTTP 500; retryable).
      enum: [OK, OPERATOR_CONFIG_ERROR, BAD_REQUEST, SIGNATURE_OR_CLOCK, RATE_LIMITED, INTERNAL]
```
